name: tree-view
description: Single-select hierarchical tree with complete WAI keyboard navigation, explicit positional ARIA, keyed-rendering support, and lazy expansion events

item.description: Tree node with role=treeitem, roving tabindex, selection state, and explicit aria-level/setsize/posinset

label.description: Visible accessible label and pointer-selection target for an item

toggle.description: Decorative disclosure indicator; clicking it toggles a parent item

group.description: Nested role=group owned by a parent treeitem; hidden while the parent is collapsed

keyboard.ArrowDown: move focus to the next visible enabled item without wrapping
keyboard.ArrowUp: move focus to the previous visible enabled item without wrapping
keyboard.ArrowRight: expand a collapsed parent, or move from an expanded parent to its first enabled child
keyboard.ArrowLeft: collapse an expanded parent, or move from a collapsed/leaf child to its enabled parent
keyboard.Home: move focus to the first visible enabled item
keyboard.End: move focus to the last visible enabled item
keyboard.Printable characters: move focus by case-insensitive typeahead with a 500ms buffer
keyboard.*: expand every enabled parent at the focused item's sibling level

notes: Only parent and data-lazy items expose aria-expanded; leaf items must not carry it.
The controller computes aria-level, aria-setsize, and aria-posinset after every keyed DOM reconciliation.
DOM focus and aria-selected remain distinct until Enter, Space, or pointer selection.
Items with aria-disabled=true are omitted from the roving focus order and cannot be selected.

faqir:select.description: Fires after click, Enter, Space, or select() establishes the single selected item

faqir:expand.description: Fires whenever a parent opens or closes; an app or l-source listener can provide children when expanded and lazy are true

api.expand: (itemOrValue) => boolean — reveal a parent or request lazy children
api.refresh: () => Element[] — recompute hierarchy, positional ARIA, selection, and roving tabindex
api.destroy: () => void — remove listeners, observer, timer, and private handle
